Japanese: ２月　９日　午前　３時４３分 堀田クリニック　集中治療室
English: February 9, 3:43 AM Hotti Clinic Intensive Care Unit

Japanese: ‥‥ぼくは今、ある裁判の記録を 目の前にしている。
English: I'm still up at this hour, reading through the trial record of a certain case.

Japanese: ぼくの師匠、千尋さんが 初めて法廷に立ったときの記録だ。
English: It's the first case my mentor, Mia, had ever handled in a court of law.

Japanese: そこには‥‥信じたくない 《真実》が、ぼくを待っていた。
English: The horrifying truth that I refuse to accept is holding me hostage here within its pages.

Japanese: ‥‥美柳　ちなみ‥‥
English: Dahlia Hawthorne...

Japanese: どうしても、信じられなかった。 記録の中に書かれているのは‥‥
English: What I have read, I don't want to believe.

Japanese: ぼくの知っている 彼女ではなかった。
English: What is written here... This wasn't the Dahlia I knew.

Japanese: ‥‥吾童川に飲みこまれたぼくは、 奇跡的に救出されたらしいけど‥‥
English: After falling into the Eagle River, I was somehow miraculously saved...

Japanese: 考えられるかぎり、この世で最も タチの悪いカゼをひいてしまった。
English: But I ended up catching a cold that seemed to knock me around the world and back.

Japanese: ‥‥目はくらみ、耳鳴りがして ノドは焼け、アタマは煮えたぎる。
English: I feel dizzy, my ears are ringing, my throat burns, and my head is on fire.

Japanese: しかし、かならず回復する！ ‥‥今日の午後までに‥‥
English: But I will recover! I have to recover by this afternoon...

Japanese: 考えられるかぎり、この世で最も タチの悪い証人を相手に‥‥
English: I have to meet with the most ill-tempered witness imaginable.

Japanese: アイツなら！　きっと なんとかしてくれるはずだから‥‥
English: But I know that he will be able to help me... somehow!

Japanese: ２月　９日　午後　２時４３分 おぼろ橋
English: February 9, 2:43 PM Dusky Bridge

Japanese: だ‥‥大丈夫なのか、成歩堂。
English: Wright... Are you sure you're well enough to be doing this?

Japanese: カオが、なんというか‥‥ ミドリ色、だが。
English: You still look a little green in the face.

Japanese: 専門的にはビリジアン、ッスね。
English: Or maybe "viridian", in artist-speak.

Japanese: ‥‥これでも、ずいぶん 熱は下がったんだけどな。
English: Actually, my fever has gone down quite a bit.

Japanese: 何度ッス？
English: How's your temperature now?

Japanese: 微熱ていどですよ。 ‥‥３８度９分だから。
English: Only 102.2 degrees... Nothing to worry about.

Japanese: げほ。げほげほがほげほ。
English: *cough* *cough* *cough* *cough*

Japanese: とにかく‥‥裁判の記録は さっき読ませてもらったよ。
English: Anyway... I read today's trial record.

Japanese: ‥‥さすが御剣だな。 あの状況で、持ちこたえるなんて。
English: You weren't bad, Edgeworth... Pretty impressive despite the circumstances.

Japanese: まだ、キケンな状況で あることはまちがいないが、な。
English: We're not in the clear yet.

Japanese: とにかく、ポイントは凶器だ。
English: The main point of contention tomorrow is going to be the murder weapon.

Japanese: “きょうき”‥‥
English: Yeah...

Japanese: 七支刀は、凶器ではなかった。
English: In the end, the Shichishito did not deliver the deadly blow.

Japanese: 我々の知らないカタナが‥‥ どこかに眠っているはずだ。
English: Which means there must be another sword hiding out there that we don't know about...

Japanese: （もう１本のカタナ、か‥‥）
English: (Another sword, huh...)

Japanese: シンパイいらねッス！
English: Don't you worry about anything, pal!

Japanese: この自分がッ！　かならず 見つけだしてみせるッス！
English: I'll dig up the murder weapon myself, or I'll eat my coat!

Japanese: ‥‥ありがとう、御剣。 あとは‥‥ぼくに、まかせてくれ。
English: ...Thanks again, Edgeworth. I'll handle things from here.

Japanese: ‥‥フッ‥‥ それもよかろう。
English: Heh... That is probably for the best.

Japanese: じつは‥‥私は一度、 署に戻ろうと思っているのだ。
English: Actually, I was thinking about paying the old precinct a little visit.

Japanese: どうしても、 調べてみたいことがある。
English: There's something I want to look into...

Japanese: なんのことだ‥‥？
English: And that is...?

Japanese: もちろん‥‥我々の 依頼人のことだよ。
English: Our client's background, naturally.

Japanese: あやめ‥‥さん、ッスか？
English: You mean Iris, sir...?

Japanese: 私は‥‥どこかで、彼女に 会ったような気がするのだ。
English: I have the feeling that we've met before.

Japanese: それを、たしかめたい。 どうやら‥‥
English: All I want is confirmation, one way or the other.

Japanese: キミの口からは、 聞けそうもないからな。
English: And since I probably won't be getting that from you...

Japanese: ‥‥すまないな、御剣。
English: I'm sorry, Edgeworth...

Japanese: それでは‥‥また、 後ほど会おう、成歩堂。
English: I bid you adieu, Wright. ...Take care.

Japanese: ‥‥さて。 じゃ、自分も行くッス。
English: Guess I should get moving, too.

Japanese: ？　どこか、行くんですか？
English: Why? Do you need to be somewhere?

Japanese: この、橋ッスよ。
English: Yeah, I've gotta get to work on this bridge, pal.

Japanese: 向こう側へわたるために 応急処置の準備をしてるッス。
English: I'm rigging something up so we can get across to the other side.

Japanese: （そうだ‥‥！　奥の院には、 真宵ちゃんが‥‥）
English: (Ack! That's right! Maya is still stuck over at the Inner Temple...)

Japanese: シンパイいらねッス！
English: But don't you worry, pal!

Japanese: わたれるようになったら、 アンタにも知らせてあげるッス。
English: As soon as it's all set, you'll be the first to know.

Japanese: あ、ありがとうございます！ イトノコ刑事！　げほげほ！
English: Th-Thank you, Gumshoe! *cough* *cough*

Japanese: げほ。げほげほがほげほ。
English: *cough* *cough* *cough*

Japanese: ま。ま。なんでもいいから、 カゼをうつさないでほしいッス。
English: No problem, pal. Just try not to give me that cough of yours, OK?

Japanese: じゃッ！　後ほど会うッス！
English: Alright, I'm off!

Japanese: （待っていてくれ、真宵ちゃん。 かならず、助けに行くから）
English: (Hang in there, Maya. We'll get you out. I promise.)

Japanese: （それまでに‥‥手がかりを 集めるんだ！）
English: (But in the meantime... I've got to continue collecting evidence!)

Japanese: 《おぼろ橋》というより、本当に 《おんぼろ橋》になっちまった。
English: Now it really is more of a "Dusty Bridge" than a "Dusky Bridge".

Japanese: １０年ほど前の誘拐事件といい、 呪われた橋、だよな‥‥
English: That kidnapping incident happened here 11 years ago, so maybe it's cursed, too.

Japanese: 葉桜院には、電話がないらしい。
English: Hazakura Temple doesn't have a single private phone line...

Japanese: このへんに民家はないから、 ビキニさん専用、なのだろうか。
English: ...and there aren't any houses in the area, so I guess Bikini is the only one who uses this.

Japanese: ‥‥携帯電話ぐらい、 もってくればよかったな。
English: ...I really wish I had brought my cell phone with me.

Japanese: あいかわらず、危なっかしく かたむいている。
English: That thing looks like it's ready to collapse... At least more than it did before.

Japanese: ビキニさんの話では、吾童山は 地震の多い土地柄らしい。
English: According to Bikini, Eagle Mountain is very prone to earthquakes.

Japanese: ‥‥そういえば‥‥
English: Which reminds me...

Japanese: 御剣のヤツ、地震が 大のニガテだったっけな‥‥
English: If my memory serves me correctly, Edgeworth isn't exactly a fan of earthquakes.

Japanese: 葉桜院に向かう方向とは別に、 １本の細い道がのびている。
English: There's a narrow path going off in a different direction than that of the Main Hall.

Japanese: 道しるべには《極楽庵へ》と 書かれているようだ。
English: Looks like someone's taken the effort to write "To Heavenly Hall" on the signpost.

Japanese: 向こう岸まで、２０メートル近く ありそうだ。
English: It looks like it's about 20 yards to that cliff over there.

Japanese: こちらから、奥の院は見えない。
English: But I can't see the Inner Temple from here.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃん、ブジだといいけど‥‥
English: I really hope Maya's OK...

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 葉桜院　山門
English: February 9 Hazakura Temple Main Gate

Japanese: だからさー、たのむよお。 オレのゲージュツのためにさあ。
English: Come on! Pleeease! It's for art's sake! I swear!

Japanese: （‥‥あれ。この、果てしなく だらしない声は‥‥）
English: (There's only one guy I know who could be this persistent and high-strung...)

Japanese: ヒロインよ、ヒロイン。 オレの絵本の。メイヨなコトだぜ？
English: I'm talking about the heroine here! The heroine in my book. It'll make you famous.

Japanese: ぎゃははァッ！
English: Aaagh!

Japanese: ‥‥クドいッ！
English: ...Enough!

Japanese: バカをバカゆえにユルすバカは、 そのバカにもおとるバカ‥‥
English: A fool's fool fools fools who foolishly accept the foolishness of a fool's fool.

Japanese: そう思わないかしら？ ‥‥成歩堂　龍一。
English: Wouldn't you agree... Phoenix Wright?

Japanese: た、たしか、キミは‥‥ ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ええと。
English: Wait a second... I-I know you... You're... Um...

Japanese: いてててッ！
English: Oww!

Japanese: ‥‥思い出すのが、おそい。
English: Your reflexes and mind need to shape up!

Japanese: （まだ、熱でアタマが はたらかないんだよ！）
English: (My brain's frying like a sunny-side up and you want to grill me over a name!?)

Japanese: ま、マズいだろ、メイちゃん！
English: Franzy! You can't do that!

Japanese: コイツ、生死の境をさまよってる、 ってウワサだぜ。‥‥今もなお。
English: Even as we speak, Nick's on the brink of death! ...Or so I'm told.

Japanese: ‥‥《死者にムチ打て！》 この国では、そう言うそうね。
English: "Whip a dead horse!" Isn't that one of your American sayings?

Japanese: 言わないよ！ ‥‥それに、死んでないし。
English: No, it's not! And I'm not on the brink of anything.

Japanese: なああ、成歩堂ォ。オマエからも 言ってやってくれよぉ。
English: Come on, Nick. Tell her, would you?

Japanese: 《メイちゃんのムチムチ大冒険》の モデルになってくれ、ってさあ。
English: She must model for my picture book, "Franzy's Whip Lash Splash: Simple & Magnificent"!

Japanese: ぎゃははァッ！
English: Gaaah!

Japanese: ‥‥私にモデルをたのむ前に、 マトモな証言をすることね。
English: Before you ask me to model, learn to give at least semi-coherent testimony.

Japanese: ‥‥その前に、マトモに 生きることだな、矢張。
English: And before that, you'll have to learn how to live a semi-coherent life, Larry.

Japanese: ダレがなんて言ったって、 オレ！　ホントに見たんだ！
English: I don't care what anyone says! I'm telling the truth! I saw what I saw!

Japanese: あのコはあの晩、ピョーン、って 飛んだんだ！　ピョーン、って！
English: She flew, I'm telling you! Whooosh! Just like that dude with the red underwear!

Japanese: あとであやまったって、 ユルしてやらねえェからな！
English: Don't think I'm gonna forgive you guys when you come crawling back to apologize!

Japanese: （‥‥やれやれ。 行っちまった‥‥）
English: (*sigh*... Off he goes...)

Japanese: ‥‥１年ぶりね、成歩堂　龍一。
English: Phoenix Wright, it's been a year since we last met. A pleasure to see you again.

Japanese: それにしても、でっかい 山門だなあ。
English: This is one impressive gate.

Japanese: ‥‥狩魔の屋敷の正門にくらべたら まだまだ、コドモね。
English: Compared to the grandeur of the main gate at the Von Karma Estate, it's but a pet door.

Japanese: 雪がつもって、ふぜいがあるし。
English: It looks really idyllic with all that snow on it, don't you think?

Japanese: ‥‥私の街では、こんなの、 降ったうちに入らないわ。
English: All that snow? Don't make me laugh. This is but a light dusting where I come from.

Japanese: ぼくがアメリカに行かないからって テキトーなコト、言うなよ。
English: Don't make things up just because you think I'll never get to see it for myself!

Japanese: ‥‥事件当夜、コイツは 何のために使用されたんだろう‥‥
English: I'd love to know what that thing was used for on the night of the crime.

Japanese: フッ‥‥。あいかわらず、レベルが 低いわね、成歩堂　龍一。
English: Hah! Still thinking small I see, Phoenix Wright. Or perhaps, not at all.

Japanese: そんなことだから、この 狩魔に勝てないのよ！
English: That's why you will never defeat me!

Japanese: 悪いけど、きみに 負けたおぼえはないぞ。
English: Sorry to burst your bubble, but I don't recall ever losing to you.

Japanese: ‥‥今度こそ、キッチリ 勝負をつける必要があるわね。
English: It's time for us to settle this once and for all with one final showdown!

Japanese: （‥‥向こうも、負けた おぼえはないらしいな‥‥）
English: (Looks like she mentally blocked out my victories over her from her memory...)

Japanese: かわいらしい鐘つき堂だ。
English: It's a quaint, little bell tower.

Japanese: あやめさんが《消灯の鐘》を 鳴らしたときは‥‥
English: I never would've thought that something this horrible was about to happen...

Japanese: まさか、こんな大変なコトに なるなんて、思わなかったのに。
English: ...when Iris rang the lights out bell that night.

Japanese: ここから、 葉桜院の本堂が見える。
English: You can see the Main Hall from here.

Japanese: ジューショクさま‥‥ 大丈夫かしら。
English: I wonder how the head nun is holding up...?

Japanese: なんだ。オバサンには やさしいんだなあ。
English: Oh, wow. Is that an inkling of human kindness I sense?

Japanese: いてててッ！
English: Oww!

Japanese: ‥‥まるで、他のヒトには やさしくないみたいな言い方ね。
English: Do you enjoy causing other people pain with that sharp tongue of yours?

Japanese: （そういうことは ムチをしまってから言えよ‥‥）
English: (It's nothing compared to the pain you cause with that leathery whip of yours...)

Japanese: あいかわらず、アメリカで？
English: Have you been in Germany all this time?

Japanese: ええ。‥‥モチロン、 カンペキに勝ちつづけているわ。
English: That's right. Extending my perfect win record. Naturally.

Japanese: （‥‥やれやれ。 変わってないみたいだな）
English: (Oh, joy. Sounds like she hasn't changed a bit.)

Japanese: （あれから、１年‥‥か）
English: (Has it really been a year since we first met...?)

Japanese: 私の名は狩魔　冥（かるまめい）。 ‥‥天才検事よ。
English: I am Franziska von Karma, the Prodigy.

Japanese: はあ‥‥。
English: I... see...

Japanese: 私がアメリカの検事局を捨てて この国へ来た理由は、ただ１つ。
English: I gave up a promising career in Germany and came to this country for one sole reason.

Japanese: ‥‥復讐よ。
English: Revenge.

Japanese: （彼女はアメリカに生まれ育ち、 １３才で検事になったという）
English: (Franziska was born and raised in Germany, and became a prosecutor at the age of 13.)

Japanese: （‥‥彼女の父親は、 伝説の検事・狩魔　豪）
English: (Her father was the legendary prosecutor, Manfred von Karma.)

Japanese: （４０年間、無敗をほこり‥‥ 今はもう、いない）
English: (He had a perfect win record for 40 long years... But now, he is gone from this world...)

Japanese: もしかして、きみは‥‥
English: Don't tell me you still hold a grudge against me...

Japanese: まだ、お父さんのことで、 このぼくを‥‥？
English: ...because of what happened to your father...

Japanese: 成歩堂　龍一。 私は、あなたを倒す‥‥かならず。
English: Phoenix Wright. You will fall before me. This, I promise.

Japanese: でも‥‥それは、私自身のため。 父は、関係ないわ。‥‥もう。
English: But it will be for my sake, not my father's... Are we clear?

Japanese: ‥‥そう、か。
English: ...Yes, crystal.

Japanese: 正直なところ、おどろいたわ。
English: In truth, I was shocked.

Japanese: 私は、キサマと闘うつもりで この国に戻ってきた。
English: I came back to America with the intention of defeating you.

Japanese: まさか‥‥弁護席に あのオトコが立とうとは、ね。
English: Instead, it was my little brother who was leading the defense.

Japanese: （御剣　怜侍。 考えてみれば、アイツも‥‥）
English: (Edgeworth... Come to think of it...)

Japanese: （あの狩魔　豪に《検事》を たたきこまれた、天才なんだ）
English: (...Edgeworth was pulled into being a prosecutor by Manfred von Karma as well.)

Japanese: 御剣　怜侍は‥‥ 私に、こう言ったわ。
English: Miles Edgeworth told me something very interesting, you know.

Japanese: 『この事件は、成歩堂にとって 特別なイミを持つ』‥‥って。
English: He said this case has a special significance to you.

Japanese: ‥‥ああ。 そのとおりだね。
English: Because it does.

Japanese: だからこそ、私は来たのよ。
English: And that's precisely why I am here.

Japanese: 特別なイミを持つ事件で、 キサマをたたきツブすために！
English: Your personal involvement will make crushing you into teensy weensy pieces all the better!

Japanese: （ここまでスナオに 敵意をむき出しにされると‥‥）
English: (It's probably the fever, but...)

Japanese: （なんか、ほほえましいな）
English: (She's so openly hostile that it's almost kinda cute.)

Japanese: いててててッ！
English: Oww!

Japanese: ニコニコするなッ！
English: No smirking!

Japanese: その前に、病人をたたくなッ！
English: No whipping the sick!

Japanese: あのオトコも、こんなバカのために あんなバカなコトをするなんてね。
English: That foolish fool, doing such a foolish favor for such a foolishly foolish fool.
(Edgeworth...)

Japanese: いいかしら、成歩堂　龍一。 私にはキタイしないコトね。
English: Make no mistake, Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: あくまでも、あなたを ブチのめしに来たわけだから。
English: I came here for one thing, and one thing only. To pulverize you.

Japanese: きみに《カゼゴロシ・Ｚ》を買いに 行ってもらおうとは思わないよ。
English: ...It's not like I thought you were here to bring me some Coldkiller X, you know.

Japanese: ‥‥事件の資料は、飛行機の中で ざっと目を通したわ。
English: I went over the whole case file on the flight over.
You read the whole thing?

Japanese: ええ。カンペキにね。
English: Yes. Every last word of every last sentence of every last paragraph.

Japanese: あなたのバカげた行動には 笑わせてもらったわ。
English: All the ridiculous things you did made it a very interesting read, you know.

Japanese: 燃えつきた橋がわたれるかどうか ‥‥考えなかったのかしら？
English: Attempting to cross a burning bridge? Did you even consider the dangers?

Japanese: 考えなかったよ。
English: No.

Japanese: あのときは、《わたる》 しかなかったからね。
English: The only thought in my mind was, "I have to get across."

Japanese: か、考えないバカは、 考えるバカよりタチが悪いようね。
English: A fool who doesn't think is more foolish than a fool who foolishly thinks.

Japanese: （橋がわたれるようになったら、 イトノコ刑事が教えてくれる）
English: (Gumshoe said he'd let me know once the bridge was repaired.)

Japanese: （真宵ちゃん‥‥ブジだよな！ 早く、ぼくに聞かせてほしい）
English: (Maya... She's got to be OK. I just know it.)

Japanese: （事件当夜、そっちでは 何があったのか‥‥？）
English: (Plus, I need to ask her about what really happened at the Inner Temple that night...)

Japanese: あの。これ、なんだけど
English: Um, about this...

Japanese: いてててッ！
English: Yeow!

Japanese: いっさい、協力はしないわ。 ‥‥成歩堂　龍一。
English: I refuse to help you, Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥）
English: (I should've known better than to try...)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 葉桜院　山門
English: February 9 Hazakura Temple Main Gate

Japanese: どうやら‥‥ 待ち人が来たようね。
English: It seems you have a visitor.

Japanese: あ‥‥成歩堂さん！
English: Mr. Wright!

Japanese: あやめさん！ よく来てくれましたね！
English: Iris! I'm really glad to see you!

Japanese: ゴドー検事が、緊急事態という ことで、警察を動かしたのだ。
English: Godot managed to mobilize the police by claiming a state of emergency.

Japanese: また、ゴドー‥‥か。
English: Not him again.

Japanese: と、とにかく急いでくれ！ 修験洞の《錠》を‥‥
English: Never mind him! We've gotta hurry and unlock the Sacred Cavern...

Japanese: ‥‥ごめんなさい、成歩堂さん！ 私のせいで、大切な方を‥‥
English: I'm sorry, Mr. Wright! It's my fault someone so important to you is locked in there...

Japanese: 検事さま。お話はあとに いたしましょう！
English: Mr. Edgeworth. Can we finish our conversation later?

Japanese: ‥‥うム。
English: ...Alright.

Japanese: では、私たちは奥の院に向かう。 ‥‥あとでまた会おう、成歩堂。
English: Let's hurry to the Inner Temple then. ...I'll see you later, Wright.

Japanese: ちょっと待ってくれ、御剣！ ぼくも行くに決まってるだろう！
English: Wait, Edgeworth! I'm coming with you guys!

Japanese: 申しわけないが‥‥ そういうワケにはいかない。
English: Sorry, Wright. I can't allow you to come with us.
What!?

Japanese: ‥‥言いにくいことだが‥‥
English: I hate to be the one to break it to you, but...

Japanese: 綾里　真宵は、今。 微妙な立場に立たされている。
English: ...Maya Fey is in a rather... delicate position.

Japanese: ‥‥なんだ‥‥？ “微妙な立場”って‥‥
English: What do you mean by a delicate position...?

Japanese: これからの活動は、《救出》で あると同時に《捜査》なのだ。
English: This isn't simply a rescue operation, Wright. It's also an investigation.

Japanese: ‥‥一般人の立ち会いは、 許可できない。
English: That being the case... we can't allow members of the public to interfere.

Japanese: み、御剣‥‥
English: C-Come on, Edgeworth...

Japanese: 結果は、まっ先に知らせる。 ‥‥わかってくれ、成歩堂。
English: If anything happens, you'll be the first to know. ...Please understand, Wright.

Japanese: でも！　真宵ちゃんは‥‥
English: But it's Maya!

Japanese: 成歩堂さん！
English: Mr. Wright!

Japanese: 真宵さまを助けるのが先です。 ‥‥ちがいますか？
English: All that matters is that Mystic Maya is rescued, correct?

Japanese: その子の言うとおりよ。 ‥‥成歩堂　龍一。
English: Listen to the voice of reason for a change, Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: これより、おぼろ橋より先への 立ち入りを禁止させてもらう。
English: From now on, consider anything beyond Dusky Bridge to be off limits.

Japanese: それでは‥‥失礼する。
English: We'll be going now... Excuse us.

Japanese: いてててッ！
English: Aaack!

Japanese: 立ち止まっているヒマはないわ、 成歩堂　龍一。
English: This is no time to be standing around, Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: キサマにはまだ、 やるべきコトがあるはずよ！
English: Aren't there other things that demand your attention?

Japanese: （そうだった、な）
English: (She's right...)

Japanese: ‥‥ありがとう。 思い出させてくれて。
English: ...Thanks. For reminding me.

Japanese: （とにかく、奥の院へ‥‥）
English: (We've gotta hurry to the Inner Temple and...)

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂ッ！
English: ...Wright!

Japanese: うわッ！　な‥‥何してるんだよ、 こんなところで！
English: Aah! Wh-What are you doing here?
Gurgh...

Japanese: わ‥‥私としたことが‥‥
English: H-How could I have...?

Japanese: ダマっていては、わからないわ！
English: How do you expect us to understand if you don't speak up!

Japanese: いててててッ！
English: Yeooowww!

Japanese: ‥‥って、 なんでぼくをたたくんだよ！
English: Hey! Why am I the one getting whipped!?

Japanese: に‥‥逃げられた。
English: Sh-She's gone...

Japanese: あの、被告人‥‥あやめさんだ。 ‥‥消えてしまった！
English: The defendant... Iris... She's... gone...

Japanese: き、消えた‥‥‥？
English: Gone where?

Japanese: “脱走”、だ。 ‥‥取り逃がしてしまった！
English: She fled... She escaped!

Japanese: なんですってェッ！
English: WHAT!?

Japanese: ‥‥やっぱり‥‥さっきの？
English: So... it was just now... during the...

Japanese: （カンペキに見える、このオトコ ‥‥御剣の、たった１つの弱点）
English: (It's easy to see Edgeworth's one and only weakness.)

Japanese: （それが‥‥“地震”）
English: (His fear of earthquakes...)

Japanese: ‥‥イヤな予感はしていたのだ。 ‥‥自分がユルせない！
English: I should've known better than to escort the defendant alone!

Japanese: 地震が起こった瞬間、目の前が まっ暗になって‥‥
English: As soon as the ground started shaking, everything went dark before my eyes and...

Japanese: 意識が、飛んでしまった‥‥！
English: ...I stupidly passed out!

Japanese: おまえ、まだ‥‥あの事件を 忘れられないのか‥‥
English: That incident... haunts you to this day, doesn't it?

Japanese: それは、彼女を逃がした 理由にはならない。
English: That's no excuse for letting the suspect get away...

Japanese: この私が‥‥被告人に脱走を ユルしてしまうなんて！
English: I can't believe I let it happen.

Japanese: 奥の院側に逃げ道はない！ 彼女は、こちらに来たはずだ。
English: Wright, the Inner Temple area is a dead end, so she only could've escaped to this side.

Japanese: ‥‥私たちは、見ていないわ。
English: We haven't seen her.

Japanese: とにかく‥‥早く、 吾童山中に検問を張らねば‥‥！
English: Then we need to get a manhunt underway now, and search Eagle Mountain from top to bottom!

Japanese: ‥‥待てよ、御剣。
English: Wait, Edgeworth.

Japanese: おまえ‥‥奥の院は調べたのか？
English: Have you thoroughly checked the Inner Temple yet?

Japanese: バカな！　その必要はない。
English: Don't be ridiculous! Why would I?

Japanese: おぼろ橋の向こうは、陸の孤島。 逃げ道など‥‥
English: The Inner Temple is like an island. There's no way to escape but to cross...

Japanese: いや‥‥。
English: No, that's not it...

Japanese: ぼくの知っている彼女は、 逃げたりはしないよ、御剣。
English: Iris isn't the type of person to run away, Edgeworth.

Japanese: ‥‥なんだと‥‥？
English: What are you talking about?

Japanese: ビキニさんが言っていた。
English: Sister Bikini mentioned something when we were with her.

Japanese: さっきの揺れで‥‥修験洞が 崩れたかもしれない、って。
English: She said that the Sacred Cavern might've caved in because of the quake.

Japanese: じゃ、じゃあ彼女は‥‥ それを、たしかめるために？
English: Then you think Iris might've gone to the Inner Temple to check on the situation...?

Japanese: 御剣。彼女は奥の院にいると思う。 ‥‥検問の必要はない。
English: Listen, Edgeworth. I'm sure she's at the Inner Temple. A manhunt is not necessary.

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂‥‥
English: Wright...

Japanese: とにかく、急ごう。 奥の院‥‥修験洞に！
English: Let's not waste any more time. We need to get to the Training Hall's Sacred Cavern now!

Japanese: わかった。行こう！
English: Alright. Let's go!

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 奥の院・修験堂内
English: February 9 Inner Temple Training Hall

Japanese: （さいわいなことに‥‥ 修験洞は、崩れていなかった）
English: (Fortunately, the Sacred Cavern didn't cave in.)

Japanese: （しかし‥‥）
（そこで、ぼくたちを 待っていたのは‥‥‥）
English: (What we found...)

Japanese: （まさに想像もしない光景だった）
English: (...was something none of us would've ever expected.)

Japanese: か‥‥からくり錠が‥‥
English: H-How can there be...

Japanese: ‥‥増えている‥‥
English: ...Even more locks...

Japanese: （い、いったい、これは‥‥）
English: (Wh-What is the meaning of this...!?)

Japanese: あやめさん‥‥！
English: Iris!

Japanese: （あやめさん‥‥教えてくれ！ いったい‥‥）
English: (Iris! Please tell me!)

Japanese: （ここでは、何が起こっていると いうんだ‥‥？）
English: (What the hell is going on!?)

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 葉桜院　本堂
English: February 9 Hazakura Temple Main Hall

Japanese: はあああああああああああああああ
English: Hmmmaaaaaaaaaaaaah...

Japanese: （‥‥深ぁいタメ息が聞こえる）
English: (...That was one long sigh!)

Japanese: ‥‥あの。ビキニさん？
English: Umm... Sister Bikini?

Japanese: あらあらあら、いたのかい！ 元気？　わは。わはははははは！
English: My my my! I didn't know you were here! How are you doing? Wa ha. Wa ha ha ha ha ha ha!

Japanese: はあああああああああああああああ
English: Hmmmaaaaaaaaaaaaah...

Japanese: ‥‥あの。ムリしないほうが。
English: Um, you don't have to pretend to be in a good mood for my sake...

Japanese: もしかして‥‥ I...
オバサン、トンでもない まちがいをしてたのかねえ‥‥。
English: I suppose I've made a terrible mess of things, haven't I?

Japanese: エリスさまを死なせてしまって、 その上、あやめまで‥‥
English: I let Mystic Elise die, and then there's Iris as well...

Japanese: （“エリスさま”、ねえ‥‥）
English: ("Mystic Elise"...? Now that I think about it... Hmm...)

Japanese: それに、修験者さまも 奥の院に閉じこめられているし‥‥
English: There's an acolyte stuck at the Inner Temple...

Japanese: あの、小さな女の子まで、 どこか行っちゃって。
English: ...and that poor little girl has gone missing, too.

Japanese: 小さな‥‥？
English: That little girl...?

Japanese: ま、まさか‥‥春美ちゃんが？
English: Y-You don't mean Pearls... do you?

Japanese: そ、そうなのよォォォ！
English: Yes, I'm afraid I do!

Japanese: 事件があった翌朝から、 姿が見えないの‥‥
English: She hasn't been seen since the morning after the incident.

Japanese: は‥‥春美ちゃんが‥‥ （それは、聞いてないぞ！）
English: Pearls? She's missing!? (Why didn't someone tell me about this earlier!?)

Japanese: 笑ってしまうほど巨大な勾玉が うっそり、まつられている。
English: There's a laughably large Magatama on the altar.

Japanese: こんなちっぽけなシロモノでも サイコ・ロックが見えるんだから、
English: If I can see Psyche-Locks with the tiny Magatama I've got...

Japanese: これだけ大きかったら、 何が見えてもフシギじゃないな。
English: ...I can only imagine what kind of power this bad boy has under his hood!

Japanese: 祭壇だ。古びているが、 キレイに手入れされている。
English: It's an altar. It looks a bit old, but someone does a good job taking care of it.

Japanese: ‥‥そういえば去年、 倉院の里でも見かけたな。
English: Speaking of altars, I remember seeing one like this in Kurain Village last year.

Japanese: やっぱり、霊力に 関係しているのだろうか。
English: I guess they really do have something to do with spiritual power after all.

Japanese: 読めないけど、まがまがしい 呪文がびっしり書かれている。
English: I bet the writing on these sliding doors are instructions for spirit channeling...

Japanese: 数学の公式をトイレに張って おぼえる、みたいなコトなのか、
English: Kinda like how we scribbled math formulas on the bathroom walls to remember them.

Japanese: それとも、一種のカンニング ペーパーみたいなものなのか‥‥
English: Except we couldn't go to the bathroom during tests... And I can't read these walls.

Japanese: 火鉢なんて、テレビの時代劇で しか見たことがなかったけど、
English: I've only seen a layout like this when I saw this really old Japanese movie on TV.

Japanese: 意外にあたたかいものだ。
English: Wow! This hibachi brazier really puts out a lot of heat!

Japanese: あの夜は、みんなでコレを囲んで、 汗だくでカレーをかきこんだのに。
English: Just thinking about how happy we were, sitting here eating pot roast and gravy... *sigh*

Japanese: ワラで編んだ 円座がつまれている。
English: It's a stack of straw zabuton cushions.

Japanese: ‥‥きっと、ここに来なかったら 一生、知らなかっただろうな。
English: Maya told me this type of zabuton is called "enza"...

Japanese: 《円座》なんてコトバ。
English: I suppose if I'd never come here, I'd have missed out on all these new things.

Japanese: うおおおおおおおおおおおおおッ！
English: Heeeeeeeeeey!

Japanese: なな、ナニ！　この、まわりの メイワクを考えないオタケビは‥‥
English: Wh-What was that jarring, inconsiderately loud yell...?

Japanese: アンタ！　こんなところで くつろいでいたッスかッ！
English: So this is where you've been! Keeping nice and warm, I see!

Japanese: イトノコ刑事！ も、もしかして、おぼろ
English: Detective! H-Have you finished repair--

Japanese: 橋ッス！　橋ッス！ とりあえず、わたったッス！
English: The bridge!? Yeah, I did! I told you I'd let you know!

Japanese: あらあらあらあらあら。 じゃあ、オバサンもひとつ。
English: Well well well! Then I'll come along with you and...

Japanese: いやいやいやいやいや！ 関係者以外、立入禁止ッス。
English: Sorry, ma'am! No unauthorized entry. Access is restricted to people involved in the case.

Japanese: ナニ言ってるんだい！
English: What was that!?

Japanese: オバサン、どこから見ても、 どっぷり関係者じゃないか！
English: I'm involved, aren't I? You couldn't get much more involved than me!

Japanese: いや、まあ‥‥ ある種、そうなんスけど‥‥
English: Well yeah, but that's not what I mea--

Japanese: ぎゃはああッ！
English: Aaah!

Japanese: ひさしぶりね。 ‥‥ヒゲ。
English: It's been a while, Scruffy...

Japanese: あ‥‥‥‥アナタはァッ！
English: Y-You're...!

Japanese: か、か、か、か、か、か、か、か、
English: Ms. v-v-v-v-v-v-

Japanese: かははあああああッ！
English: Von Karmaaaaaaah!

Japanese: ジューショクさま。 調査は、私にまかせなさい！
English: Sister. Please, leave the investigation to me.

Japanese: おやおや。まあまあ。 なんか、たのもしいわ、アナタ。
English: My my my. Well, you certainly seem to have everything under control!

Japanese: 私は、あなたのミカタ。 ‥‥悪いようにはしないわ。
English: I am on your side. I won't do anything you don't want me to.

Japanese: （あやめさんを有罪に しようとしているクセに‥‥）
English: (...As you work to pin the guilt on Iris.)

Japanese: ナニをぼやぼやしているの、 成歩堂　龍一ッ！
English: What are you daydreaming about, Phoenix Wright!?

Japanese: い、イタいな！　ナニするんだ
English: OWW! That hurts, you know!

Japanese: おダマりッ！
English: Silence!

Japanese: この狩魔が、キサマの調査の 指導をしてあげるわ。
English: I, Franziska von Karma, will personally guide you through your investigation.

Japanese: さっさと、ついて来なさいッ！
English: So you will follow me!

Japanese: （や‥‥やれやれ‥‥ ついてくる気か‥‥このムスメ）
English: (...She's not seriously going to follow me around, is she...?)

Japanese: ‥‥オバサンも、トシかねえ。
English: I must be getting old...

Japanese: なんだか、自分に自信が 持てなくなってきたのよ。
English: I think I've seriously lost faith in myself.

Japanese: 今日の法廷、ですか‥‥？
English: Are you talking about your performance at the trial today?

Japanese: オバサン、アレよ？ ウソなんて、ついてないんだから！
English: You believe me, don't you? I'm not a liar! I would never lie!

Japanese: あの晩、あの子が‥‥エリスさまの 身体から、ぬーっとカタナを‥‥
English: I know what I saw! I saw Iris pull that sword from Mystic Elise's body that night...

Japanese: オバサン、ホントに見たんだから！ ‥‥今朝までは、そう思ってたわ。
English: I'm certain of it! At least, I was until this morning...

Japanese: （サイコ・ロックが現れない‥‥ たしかに、ウソはついていない）
English: (I don't see any Psyche-Locks, so she must be telling the truth.)

Japanese: あの。‥‥どうして、急に自信を なくしちゃったんですか？
English: Um... So why are you so unsure of yourself all of a sudden?

Japanese: オバサンのあとに証言した、 あのゲージュツカがいたでしょ。
English: You know that artist who testified after me?

Japanese: あやめちゃんが、飛んだんだよ！ 白いずきんをハタめかせて！
English: I saw Iris flying! Her white hood fluttering!

Japanese: なんだか、オレも 飛べそうな気がしてさァ。
English: I felt like I might start flying myself.

Japanese: ‥‥あり得ないコトを必死で しゃべってる、あの子を見て‥‥
English: When I saw that man testify so fervently about something so impossible...

Japanese: ああ、オバサンもしょせん あのテイドなのかな、って。
English: ...I started to wonder if I had acted just like him when I was on the witness stand.

Japanese: ‥‥まあ、あいつのコトは 気にしないほうがいいですよ。
English: I wouldn't take that guy too seriously.

Japanese: ゲージュツカと言っても、 トラブル関係の芸術ですから。
English: He's an artist, but all he draws is trouble... and nothing else.

Japanese: （ビキニさんも、矢張も‥‥ ウソはついていない）
English: (If both Bikini and Larry are telling the truth...)

Japanese: （考えられるのは‥‥ 《見まちがい》‥‥かな）
English: (...that can only mean one thing. They both didn't see what they think they saw.)

Japanese: 事件当夜‥‥奥の院で、 あやめさんと会ったんですよね？
English: On the night of the incident, you met Iris at the Inner Temple. Is that correct?

Japanese: そうだよ。あれはたしかに あやめだったワ。
English: That's right. I'm sure it was Iris.

Japanese: （でも‥‥あやめさんは、葉桜院の 部屋にいた、と言っている‥‥）
English: (But Iris claims she was in her room in Hazakura Temple.)

Japanese: やっぱり‥‥あの晩、こっちに 戻ったのは、マチガイだったねえ。
English: I knew I shouldn't have come back here that night...

Japanese: おかげで真宵ちゃん、ひとりで 閉じこめられているんですよね。
English: But because you did, Maya is stuck at the Inner Temple.

Japanese: うううう‥‥申しワケないねえ。
English: Nngh... I am so sorry.

Japanese: あそこ、スキマ風がシャレに ならないのよ。‥‥特に、冬は。
English: The drafts in that place are nothing to shake a stick at. Winter is especially bad.

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥あの修験堂、 今にもくずれそうだったな）
English: (I'll bet. The Training Hall looks like it's about to fall down any second.)

Japanese: 吾童山はね。ムカシから 大きな地震が多いのよ。
English: Eagle Mountain has always been prone to earthquakes, just so you know.

Japanese: 地震‥‥ですか‥‥
English: E-Earthquakes...?

Japanese: 大きいのが来たら、今度は つぶれちまうかもしれないねェ。
English: Yes. I wouldn't be surprised if the next big one levels the Training Hall.

Japanese: （‥‥一刻も早く 助け出さないとな‥‥）
English: (We've got to get Maya out of there... fast!)

Japanese: まあ、でも。とりあえず シンパイいらないと思うわよ。
English: But still. There's really no need to worry...

Japanese: あそこは、いわば 陸の孤島だから。
English: That area on the other side of Dusky Bridge is isolated like an island.

Japanese: 《陸の孤島》‥‥？
English: It's like an island? How so?

Japanese: そう。おぼろ橋の向こうには、 奥の院しかないのよォ。
English: Well, the only thing on that side of the bridge is the Inner Temple.

Japanese: 川とガケと森に囲まれててね。 人は住んでないんだわ。
English: No one lives out there, and it's surrounded on all sides by the river or the forest.

Japanese: そ、そうなんですか。
English: I-I see...

Japanese: だから、もし怪しいヤツが 向こう側に逃げこんだとしても‥‥
English: So a criminal would be trapped should they choose to flee in that direction.

Japanese: おぼろ橋がないかぎり、どこにも 逃げられないから、アンシンよ。
English: And as long as the bridge is out of commission, he or she will have to stay there.

Japanese: それって、真宵ちゃんも逃げ場が ない、ってコトじゃないですか！
English: That means Maya could be stuck out there with a murderer and no way to escape either!

Japanese: わわ！　そりゃそうだわ！
English: Oh dear! Yes, I suppose it does!

Japanese: （うううう‥‥早く わたれるようにならないかな）
English: (Nngh... Please, Gumshoe! Get that bridge up faster!)

Japanese: ‥‥ちょっと、気になる コトがあるんですけど。
English: ...I'm curious about a few things, if you don't mind answering some questions.

Japanese: え、なんだろ？ オバサンのスリーサイズかしら？
English: Oh? Do you need to know my measurements for your investigation or something?

Japanese: いやいや。
English: No, no, no!

Japanese: 被害者の天流斎　エリス 先生のコトです。
English: I want to know a little more about the victim, Ms. Elise Deauxnim.

Japanese: さすがに、オバサンも知らないよ。 エリスさまのスリーサイズは。
English: I'm afraid I don't know her waist size. Or her bust size for that matter.

Japanese: 彼女は‥‥なぜ、こんなところに 泊まりに来たんでしょう。
English: No, no. I'm wondering why she came to stay in a place like this to begin with.

Japanese: ‥‥修行をするワケでもないのに。
English: I mean, she told us herself that she wasn't here for spiritual training.

Japanese: あ‥‥あらあら。“こんなところ” とは、ごアイサツねえ。
English: My my my... You make it sound like this place is some sort of dump, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: え、エリスさまは、吾童山のキツい 自然を取材なさっていたのよぉ。
English: Mystic Elise was here to soak up the natural beauty of Eagle Mountain, if you must know.

Japanese: ‥‥それに、ビキニさん‥‥
English: There. You did it again... Sister Bikini...

Japanese: 彼女のこと、“エリスさま”って 呼びますよね。
English: I noticed you always refer to Ms. Deauxnim as "Mystic Elise".

Japanese: どうしてですか？ 彼女は、修験者でもないのに。
English: Yes. Now, why is that? After all, she's not here as an acolyte...

Japanese: そそ、そんな！ た、ただのお客さんだよ！
English: W-We address all our visitors as "Mystic". It makes their experience feel authentic.

Japanese: だ、だって‥‥ホラ！ 年上だから。オバサンより。
English: A-And anyway... She's older than me. You must respect your elders, you know.

Japanese: ‥‥どうして知ってるんですか？ 彼女が“年上”だって。
English: How do you know that? How can you say for sure that she's older than you?

Japanese: （どうやら‥‥）
（天流斎　エリス先生は、 ただのお客じゃなかったようだ）
English: (It seems Ms. Elise Deauxnim was no ordinary visitor after all...)

Japanese: （それに‥‥）
English: (And there's Pearls...)

Japanese: （事件当夜、彼女といっしょにいた はずの春美ちゃんが、消えた）
English: (She was with Ms. Deauxnim on the evening of the murder, and now she's vanished.)

Japanese: （‥‥何か、関係が あるはずだよな‥‥）
English: (It's all got to be connected somehow...)

Japanese: ビキニさん。
English: Tell me, Sister Bikini...

Japanese: 天流斎　エリス先生は、 どういう方だったんですか？
English: What kind of person was Ms. Deauxnim?

Japanese: 霊行道の修行をするワケでもなく、 あなたは“エリスさま”と呼ぶ。
English: I mean, you call her "Mystic Elise" even though she wasn't here for training.

Japanese: この葉桜院にとって‥‥、何か 特別なイミのある人なんですか？
English: Does she have some special connection with Hazakura Temple that I'm not aware of?

Japanese: 悪いけど‥‥ アナタには、お話しできないわ。
English: I'm terribly sorry, but... I just can't talk about it.

Japanese: （じつは‥‥）
（少し、イヤな予感がしている。 エリス先生のコト‥‥）
English: (There's a nasty feeling building in the pit of my stomach...)

Japanese: （‥‥サイコ・ロックを 解除するしかない、な‥‥）
English: (Looks like I'm going to have to break this Psyche-Lock and find out the truth.)

Japanese: ま、まさか‥‥ 春美ちゃんまで！
English: Wh-Why did Pearls have to get mixed up in this mess!?

Japanese: おねがいよォ！　そんな悲しそうな 目をしないでちょうだい！
English: Please, Mr. Wright! I know you're worried, but try to keep it together!

Japanese: （まいったな‥‥ アタマがガンガンしてきた！）
English: (Oh, man... My head's throbbing so bad, it's killing me!)

Japanese: 春美ちゃん‥‥事件当夜は、 エリス先生といっしょ、でしたね？
English: Pearls... She was with Ms. Deauxnim on the night of the murder, remember?

Japanese: さあ‥‥。オバサンたちは 修行のしたくがあったから‥‥
English: Yes, but... I have a temple to run, you know. I was busy preparing for the training...

Japanese: 夕食後、あの子は 見てないんだけど。
English: I didn't see the little darling even once after we'd finished eating dinner.

Japanese: （あの事件‥‥。まさか、 春美ちゃんの目の前で‥‥？）
English: (The murder... It didn't take place right in front of her innocent eyes, did it...?)

Japanese: 刑事さんの話では、 おうちにも帰ってないんだって。
English: According to the detective, she hasn't turned up at her home, either.

Japanese: （お、落ちつくんだ！ １カ所だけ‥‥残っている！）
English: (Come on! Keep calm! There's one place left where Pearls could be!)

Japanese: （１カ所だけ‥‥あの子が いるかもしれない場所が！）
English: (She just has to be there!)

Japanese: （‥‥イトノコ刑事‥‥ たのむから、早く来てくれ！）
English: (Gumshoe... I'm counting on you to check it out for me, so please hurry the repairs up!)

Japanese: オバサンのせいなのよねぇ‥‥ おもに、オバサンのコシのせい。
English: It's all my fault, isn't it? It's all my achy back's fault.

Japanese: エリスさまが亡くなって、 修験者さまも閉じこめられて‥‥
English: Now Mystic Elise is dead, and an acolyte is trapped inside the Inner Temple...

Japanese: 小さな女の子も愛想をつかして、 どこか行っちゃって。
English: ...and that poor little girl has gone missing, too.

Japanese: （“どこか”ですむ モンダイじゃないけど‥‥）
English: ("Missing"...? I hope that's all it is...)

Japanese: ‥‥まだまだ。オバサン、 修行が足りないのねえ‥‥。
English: Oh dear, oh dear. I've got to train to withstand more pain, don't I...?

Japanese: ダイジなお客さまね、この子は。 エラい修験者さま。
English: She's a very important visitor, you know! An honored acolyte!

Japanese: 知ってるんですか？ 真宵ちゃんのこと。
English: She is? Do you know her or something?

Japanese: 《綾里》といえば、倉院本流の 家元の名字だからねぇ。
English: The Fey name is synonymous with the Kurain Channeling Technique.

Japanese: きっと、チカラのある 霊媒師さんになるハズよぉ。
English: Therefore, she must be a spirit medium of great power indeed.

Japanese: （どうやら、ビキニさん‥‥ 知らないみたいだな）
English: (Looks like Bikini is in the dark.)

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんが、家元の ムスメだってコト‥‥）
English: (She doesn't seem to know that Maya is the daughter of the Master of Kurain.)

Japanese: ああ‥‥この子。 どこへ行ったのかしらねェ‥‥
English: Oh dear. I wonder where on Earth this little girl has wandered off to?

Japanese: この葉桜院の近くにも いないのよぉ‥‥。
English: She's nowhere near the Main Hall. That's for sure.

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんにつづいて、 春美ちゃんまで‥‥）
English: (First I lost Maya, and now Pearls...)

Japanese: （どこまでぼくを 苦しめるんだ、この事件は！）
English: (How much more pain is this case going to cause me!?)

Japanese: （‥‥ううううう。 熱で、アタマもいたいし‥‥）
English: (Urgh... Not to mention this fever is giving me a killer migraine...)

Japanese: オバサン‥‥どうやって おわびをしたらいいのか‥‥
English: How... How can I ever make up for this...?

Japanese: おわび‥‥ですか？
English: Make up for what?

Japanese: ‥‥あんなダイジな方を 死なせてしまうなんて‥‥！
English: For allowing harm to come to such an important person!

Japanese: （やっぱり‥‥）
English: (So I was right...)

Japanese: （ただの泊まり客じゃ なかったんだな‥‥エリス先生）
English: (Ms. Deauxnim wasn't just any ol' visitor here...)

Japanese: ね。ね。アンタ！ どーなのよ、あやめは！
English: Please, tell me! What's going to happen to poor Iris!?

Japanese: ユーザイにしたら、もお‥‥
English: If she was to be found guilty, I'd...

Japanese: ショウチしないからねッ！
English: Well, I wouldn't accept it!

Japanese: それは‥‥ビキニさんの 証言しだい、だったのでは‥‥
English: But your testimony, Sister Bikini...

Japanese: しょうがないでしょ！ 見ちゃったんだから！
English: I can't help my testimony! I saw what I saw!

Japanese: それをなんとかするのが、 プロってモンでしょうが！
English: It's up to a pro lawyer like you to deal with problems like that!

Japanese: まさか、あんな どうしようもない子だとはね！
English: What a disgraceful young man!

Japanese: ‥‥やっと わかってもらえましたか。
English: You've finally seen through him, huh...

Japanese: あんな、ウソの絵まで描いて！ “目立とう精神”ってヤツかねェ！
English: How could he sit there and claim he really saw what he drew in that picture?

Japanese: まあ、オバサンの似顔絵は なかなか上手だったケド。
English: Although, the portrait he did of me was rather flattering, I must say.
(Still...)

Japanese: （アイツは、女の子をヒドい目に あわせるウソはつかない）
English: (It's not like Larry to make something up that might get a woman he likes in trouble.)

Japanese: （‥‥きっと、このスケッチには イミがあると思うんだけど‥‥）
English: (There's more to that sketch of his than meets the eye. I'm sure of it.)

Japanese: まったく、アンタの友だちは ヘンなのばっかりだね！
English: You certainly have some unusual friends, don't you, Mr. Wright! Ho ho ho!

Japanese: と、ともだち‥‥
English: F-Friends...?

Japanese: あのニヤけた絵描きはともかく、 裁判長をたたくのはマズいでしょ。
English: That fancy-pansy painter is one thing, but whipping the judge is just bad form.

Japanese: ‥‥あの、それ‥‥
English: Well... Um...

Japanese: 本人に、よおく 言って聞かせてもらえませんか。
English: Do you think you could go drill that into her head for me...? Please?

Japanese: いやー、いいセンパイを持って シアワセねぇ、アンタ。
English: You must be very happy to have such a fine mentor.

Japanese: ‥‥センパイ？ コイツ、友だちですけど‥‥
English: Mentor...? No, he's just a friend...

Japanese: あらあら。アンタったら。 またまた。ジョーダンばっかり。
English: Oh, you naughty devil! Always the joker, aren't you? Ho ho ho ho!

Japanese: どう見ても、たよりになりそう だったよ、こっちのほうが。
English: Ah, he seemed like a lovely young man... Like the kind you can always count on.

Japanese: そそ、そうですか‥‥ （うう‥‥ちょっとだけ、悲しい）
English: Y-Yeah... (It's kinda depressing how much faith she has in him...)

Japanese: あの‥‥。この写真ですけど‥‥
English: Um, about this picture...

Japanese: ああ、これねえ‥‥。
English: Oh, that old thing!

Japanese: いつもなら、断っちゃうのよ。 取材なんて。
English: I always turned those reporter-types away in the past.

Japanese: 今回は、あやめが“どうしても” って言うモンだから。
English: But Iris was so insistent this time.

Japanese: あやめさんが‥‥？
English: Iris?

Japanese: ホラ。あの子、意外に 目立ちたがり屋さんだから。
English: Oh, yes! Iris loves to be the center of attention, you know.

Japanese: （それは、あまりに 意外すぎるぞ‥‥）
English: (Really? I find that kinda hard to believe...)

Japanese: あ‥‥あらあらあら。 よく見つけたわねぇ！
English: Huh... Ahh! Well, well, well... Look what you've found!

Japanese: 倉院流霊媒道の現家元、 綾里　舞子さまよ。
English: That's the Master of the Kurain Channeling Technique, Mystic Misty Fey!

Japanese: （これが‥‥真宵ちゃんの おかあさん、か‥‥）
English: (So this woman is Maya's mother, huh...)

Japanese: 舞子さま、姿を消してから、 もう２０年近くたつのよぉ。
English: It's been nearly 20 years since Mystic Misty disappeared.

Japanese: どうやら、自分のムスメさんに 家元をゆずろうとしてるみたい。
English: Apparently, she intended to pass the torch on to her daughter.

Japanese: “ムスメさん”‥‥って、 ビキニさん、知らないんですか？
English: Her daughter? Don't you know who her daughter is?

Japanese: ま、まあ。オバサンもいちおう 倉院流の分家なんだけどね。
English: Well, I myself am part of a branch family of the Fey clan.

Japanese: 本家のコトなんて、教えて もらえないのよねぇ‥‥。
English: But even I am not privy to that kind of information when it concerns the main family...

Japanese: （ビキニさん‥‥真宵ちゃんを 知らないのか‥‥）
English: (Hmm, Bikini doesn't seem to know Maya's standing within the Fey family...)

Japanese: まあまあまあまあ！ 《退魔のずきん》じゃない。
English: Well, well, well! That's a Demon-Warding Hood!

Japanese: 修験者さまは、悪霊に ねらわれやすいから。
English: Acolytes are highly susceptible to possession by evil spirits, you know.

Japanese: オバサンたち、いつも これをかぶっているワケよぉ。
English: That's why we always wear these for protection.

Japanese: 事件当夜、あやめさんに もらったんです。
English: Iris gave this to me on the night of the murder.

Japanese: ‥‥あれから、川に落ちて‥‥
English: But then I went and fell into the river and got this nasty fever...

Japanese: ずっと、これをかぶってたら、 早く熱が下がった気がしました！
English: I thought maybe if I were to wear this hood, it'd make my fever go down faster!

Japanese: 気のせいでしょ。 カゼと霊力は、カンケイないし。
English: I don't think so. Colds and spirit power have nothing to do with each other.

Japanese: （ユメがないなあ‥‥）
English: (Way to crush a man's hope.)

Japanese: オバサンの、目のサッカク だったのかねえ。
English: Maybe my eyes were playing tricks on me.

Japanese: あやめが、あんなオソロシイ ことをするなんて‥‥
English: I mean, Iris could never do such a wicked thing.

Japanese: 今日の法廷で、 立証されたんですよね。
English: But they proved it in court today, didn't they?

Japanese: このカタナが凶器ではない、って。
English: That this sword wasn't the murder weapon.

Japanese: そうみたいだねえ‥‥。
English: That's what they were saying, yes.

Japanese: （でも‥‥このカタナは、 ぼくも目撃している‥‥）
English: (The problem is, I saw it, too...)

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥エリスさんは、 このカタナに刺されたはずだ）
English: (I'm sure that what I saw was this blade stabbed into Ms. Deauxnim's body.)

Japanese: エリスさまのツエ、だね。
English: Ah, it's Mystic Elise's staff.

Japanese: たしかに‥‥水晶が なくなってるよ。
English: Hmm... The crystal sphere really is missing from it.

Japanese: 水晶は、おぼろ橋の 近くで見つかったんですよね。
English: But the sphere was found near Dusky Bridge, right?

Japanese: ‥‥エリスさま、あの晩は 葉桜院にいたはずなのにねえ。
English: Yes. Even though I'm sure Mystic Elise was in Hazakura Temple that entire evening.

Japanese: この写真ですけど‥‥。
English: About this photograph...

Japanese: ‥‥まちがいなく、ウチの スノーモービルの跡だね。
English: Oh, yes. Those are our snowmobile's tracks, alright. I'd know them anywhere!

Japanese: （１本しか跡がないんだから‥‥）
English: (But there's only one set... Which means...)

Japanese: （雪が降っているあいだに 出かけて、やんでから戻った）
English: (...the rider went out while it was still snowing, and came back after it had stopped.)

Japanese: （そう考えるしか、ないよなあ）
English: (That's really the only logical explanation, I suppose.)

Japanese: ‥‥ホント、世も末だねえ。
English: Honestly! It's like the end of the world.

Japanese: まったくですね。
English: It's not a good thing, that's for sure.

Japanese: ‥‥こんなヘタな絵で、 ゲージュツカ、なんて。
English: To think people have the gall to call this kind of scribbling by the name of art!

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: こんなんでいいなら、オバサンも 今日から歌姫だよ！
English: If that's the sort of standard you need for the arts these days, I could be a pop diva!

Japanese: ‥‥あの。描いてある 内容のほうは‥‥？
English: Um, sure. But what about the contents of the picture? What do you make of that?

Japanese: ‥‥やっぱり、世も末だねえ。
English: It's definitely the end of the world.

Japanese: この水晶‥‥
English: This crystal sphere...

Japanese: 《退魔のずきん》のものじゃない。 ちょっと、似てるけど。
English: It's not from a Demon-Warding Hood, but I would guess it serves a similar purpose.

Japanese: やっぱり‥‥エリス先生のツエに ついていたもの、ですか。
English: If it's not from a hood, then I guess it really is from Ms. Deauxnim's staff.

Japanese: 血ィがついてるのよねえ。 かわいそうなエリスさま‥‥
English: Look at the blood on it. Oh, poor Mystic Elise...

Japanese: あの。いかがでしょうか‥‥
English: I was wondering about this...

Japanese: ‥‥そおねえ。 どうかしらねえ‥‥
English: Hmm... Let me see...

Japanese: ホラ。オバサン、アレだから。 おじょーさまだからねェ。
English: Well, as you may know... In order to see reality for what it truly is...

Japanese: 世間知らずのモノ知らず、 恥知らずで親知らずなワケでしょ。
English: ...we strive to break our attachments to much of the transient, material realm.

Japanese: “ハコ入りムスメ”って言うの？
English: I guess you could call me an "immaterial girl"!

Japanese: （きっと、小さなハコにギュウと 詰めこまれてたんだろうな）
English: (I'm guessing she makes an exception for fine dining...)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 葉桜院　本堂
English: February 9 Hazakura Temple Main Hall

Japanese: （ビキニさん、さっきより ちっぽけに見えるぞ）
English: Sister Bikini looks like she's shrunk some since I last saw her...

Japanese: “小さく”と言いなさい、 成歩堂　龍一。
English: Don't be so rude, Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥どうしたのかしら？ ジューショクさま。
English: What's wrong, Sister Bikini?

Japanese: あらあらあらあら、アンタたち。 元気？　わは。わはははははは！
English: My my my! Hello, you two! How are you doing? Wa ha. Wa ha ha ha ha ha ha!

Japanese: はあああああああああああああああ
English: Hmmmaaaaaaaaaaaaah...

Japanese: （また、落ちこんでるみたいだ）
English: (From the sound of that sigh, I guess she's still pretty down.)

Japanese: さっき、あやめが来たのよぉ。 赤い王子さまをしたがえて。
English: Iris was just here, you know. She was accompanied by that handsome, crimson-clad prince!

Japanese: （‥‥御剣のことか？）
English: (Is she talking about Edgeworth?)

Japanese: アイサツだけだ、って‥‥ 話はさせてくれなかったけど。
English: It was just a courtesy call. I wasn't allowed to talk with her at all.

Japanese: ‥‥オバサンのせいで、 こんなコトになっちまったのに。
English: Oh dear. It's all my fault that this has happened.

Japanese: あやめときたら‥‥オバサンの コシをシンパイしてくれて！
English: Poor Iris. She was worried about my back, would you believe?

Japanese: オバサン、なんてコト しちまったんだろうねえ‥‥
English: What have I done to her...?

Japanese: ‥‥それは、しかたないわ、 ジューショクさま。
English: Only what you had to do, Sister.

Japanese: あなたは、見たことから逃げずに、 それを正直に証言してくれた。
English: You bravely and truthfully testified about what you saw. There's no shame in that.

Japanese: 我々は、そのコトバを決して ムダにはしない！
English: And we'll see to it that your testimony wasn't made in vain!

Japanese: そう言ってくれるのは アナタだけよぉぉぉ‥‥
English: Well, I don't hear anyone else saying that.

Japanese: そっちの青いニイちゃんは 言ってくれないんだねぇ‥‥
English: Mr. Blue-Suits doesn't seem to share your opinion, I see.

Japanese: え。ぼくですか
English: Huh? Me?

Japanese: ‥‥いてててッ！
English: ...Ack!

Japanese: ジロリ。
English: *glare*

Japanese: り‥‥リッパだったと思いますよ。 ビキニさんは。‥‥とっても！
English: O-Oh, yes! You did great, Sister Bikini. ...Absolutely spectacular!

Japanese: そう言ってくれるのは‥‥ アナタたちだけよォォォォい！
English: *sniffle* You two are the only ones who've said that to meeee!

Japanese: （なんか、《言わされた感》が ぬぐい去れないな）
English: (I can't shake the feeling that I just became a shill to a shrew.)

Japanese: それでいいの。成歩堂　龍一。
English: Do you have a problem, Phoenix Wright?

Japanese: （‥‥たしかに、正直に 証言するには勇気が必要だ）
English: (Sure, she was brave to testify so truthfully like that.)

Japanese: （‥‥でも、ビキニさんは まだ、かくしているコトがある）
English: (But I know she's still hiding something.)

Japanese: （《サイコ・ロック》‥‥ 解除するときが来たかな）
English: (Guess it's time to break that Psyche-Lock of hers...)

Japanese: ‥‥そ、その事情というのは？
English: ...W-What happened?

Japanese: それは‥‥‥
English: Well...

Japanese: な‥‥何かしら‥‥これ‥‥
English: Wh-What's happening?

Japanese: （ま、まさか‥‥）
English: (I-It can't be...)

Japanese: じ、地震よおお‥‥ッ！
English: E-Earthquake!

Japanese: うわわわわわわわわわわわわわわわ
English: Aaaaaaaargh!

Japanese: た‥‥タイヘン！ この揺れ‥‥奥の院が危ないよ！
English: Oh my goodness! The Inner Temple! This kind of tremor might...

Japanese: お、奥の院が‥‥？
English: The Inner Temple?

Japanese: 修験洞が‥‥崩れちまうかも！
English: The Sacred Cavern in the Training Hall! It might very well cave in!

Japanese: な‥‥なんだって‥‥！
English: WH-WHAT!?

Japanese: ‥‥真宵ちゃん！‥‥
English: MAYA!!!

Japanese: ‥‥おさまった‥‥みたいね‥‥
English: It... It seems to have... passed...

Japanese: 何してるんだ！　行こう！
English: Well, we can't just stand here and do nothing! Let's go!

Japanese: ど‥‥どこへ‥‥？
English: What? Where?

Japanese: 奥の院だよ！　決まってるだろ！
English: To the Inner Temple, of course! Where else!?

Japanese: ビキニさん！　話はまた、 あとで聞かせてください！
English: Sister Bikini! We'll finish this later, alright!?

Japanese: ‥‥わかってる。 早く、行っといで！
English: Of course. You run along now!

Japanese: ケガ人が出たかもしれない‥‥ オバサンも、用意しておくよ！
English: People may have been hurt. I must get some first aid kits ready.

Japanese: じゃあ行くわよ、成歩堂　龍一！
English: Come on then, Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: 捜査現場の連中も、この狩魔が いれば、通さざるを得ない。
English: They won't bar you from entering as long as I'm with you.

Japanese: あ‥‥ありがとう。
English: Th-Thank you...

Japanese: （‥‥そのとき‥‥ イヤな予感がムネをよぎった）
English: (Right at that moment, I had the worst feeling in my gut.)
(We just had an earthquake...)

Japanese: （だいじょうぶだったかな？ ‥‥アイツ）
English: (I wonder if he's alright...)

Japanese: 御剣　怜侍のこと‥‥かしら？
English: You look worried. ...Are you thinking about Miles Edgeworth?

Japanese: とにかく、急ごう。
English: Come on. Let's go!

Japanese: ‥‥エリスさまが葉桜院に いらしたのは、１週間ほど前よ。
English: Mystic Elise graced us with her presence about a week ago.

Japanese: 《紋章の護符》を見せられて、 もお‥‥キゼツするかと思った。
English: When she showed me the talisman with that mark on it... Well! I nearly fainted!

Japanese: １７年前に姿を消されてから、 姿を見た者はいなかったんだから！
English: No one had seen her in 17 years. That's when she disappeared.

Japanese: ジューショクさま。 その《お守り》は、いったい‥‥？
English: What is the significance of this talisman, Sister?

Japanese: 《紋章の護符》と言ってね。 家元の身分を証明するアカシなの。
English: The Kurain Talisman? It's the symbol of the Master of Kurain.

Japanese: その伝承者は、死ぬまで。 身につけていなければならないわ。
English: The bearer must carry it with them always until their death.

Japanese: 死ぬまで‥‥ね。
English: Until their death...?

Japanese: ‥‥やっぱり、エリス先生は‥‥ 真宵ちゃんの、おかあさん‥‥
English: So Elise Deauxnim was really Maya's mother, huh...

Japanese: 倉院流霊媒道の家元、 綾里　舞子さまよ。
English: She was Misty Fey, the Master of the Kurain Channeling Technique.

Japanese: （‥‥そ、そうなのか‥‥）
English: (...The truth comes out at last. It looks like we're making some headway here...)

Japanese: ジューショクさま。
English: Tell me, Sister...

Japanese: １７年間も姿をかくしていた 彼女が、なぜ、今になって‥‥？
English: After 17 years, why did she choose to show herself now...?

Japanese: それには‥‥やはり、 事情があったみたいなの。
English: Because something happened that called her out of hiding.

Japanese: あの、ビキニさん。 ちょっと、これを見て
English: Um, would you mind taking a look at this, Sister Bikini?

Japanese: ぎゃあああああ！
English: Aaaah!

Japanese: ダイジな掛け軸に、 なな、なんてコトするの！
English: Our scroll! Our precious scroll! What on earth have you done to it!?

Japanese: いやいや！　ぼくじゃありませ
English: No, no! It wasn't m--

Japanese: そこのムスメさん！ ジマンのムチで、やっておしまい！
English: You there, Ms. von Karma! Time to take out that whip of yours and teach him a lesson!

Japanese: いいわ、ジューショクさま。
English: With pleasure, Sister.

Japanese: コラ！　いけない子！ 成歩堂　龍一ッ！
English: Bad boy! You need to be taught a lesson, Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: （ひいいい‥‥助けてくれぇ‥‥）
English: (Aaaaah!! Someone... Anyone... ...h...e...l...p...!)

Japanese: あの、ビキニさん。 ちょっと、これを見て
English: Um, Sister Bikini. Would you mind taking a look at this?

Japanese: ぎゃあああああああああああッ！
English: Aaaaah!

Japanese: なな、なんでアナタがそれをッ！
English: What are you doing with that!?

Japanese: 早く返しなさいッ！
English: Give it back at once!

Japanese: ダメですよ！ 証拠なんですから。
English: I can't! This is evidence.

Japanese: （やはり‥‥これは 重要なものらしいな‥‥）
English: (This really must be one very important item...)

Japanese: ～天流斎エリス～
English: -- Elise Deauxnim --

Japanese: ‥‥ビキニさん。ぼくにも、 そろそろ見えてきたんです。
English: Sister Bikini. The truth is becoming increasingly clear to me.

Japanese: 天流斎　エリス先生が、 この葉桜院にとって‥‥
English: I'm convinced that Ms. Elise Deauxnim had a special significance to this temple.

Japanese: どういうイミをもつ人物か？ ‥‥その《正体》について。
English: As to why she had a special significance, I believe it has to do with her true identity.

Japanese: ナニを言い出すのかしら この子は‥‥！
English: What on Earth are you talking about? She... She was...

Japanese: え、エリス先生は、 エリス先生は‥‥絵本作家よ！
English: M-Mystic Elise was an author. Just an author... of picture books.

Japanese: じつを言えば‥‥
English: To be honest...

Japanese: かなり早い時点から、 ある“予感”があったんです。
English: I had my suspicions almost immediately after I met her.

Japanese: よ‥‥よかん‥‥？
English: S-Suspicions?

Japanese: あとは、それをウラづける 証拠があればよかった‥‥。
English: Yes. But I didn't have any evidence to support my theory, though.

Japanese: 彼女の《正体》を物語る、 決定的な証拠が！
English: But now I do. This piece of evidence proves Ms. Deauxnim's true identity!

Japanese: いかがですか？ ‥‥ビキニさん！
English: Well, Sister Bikini!? What do you have to say to that?

Japanese: ‥‥やっと、わかったわ‥‥
English: Of course! I don't know why it didn't hit me before.

Japanese: きっと、あなたのココロは 汚れきっているの。
English: Your heart. It's impure! It's riddled with wickedness and fallacies.
Huh?

Japanese: だから、そんな愚かしい コトを口走るのね‥‥
English: That's why you keep coming up with these funny ideas.

Japanese: エリス先生の『まほうのびん』を お読みなさいッ！
English: I recommend Mystic Elise's book, "The Magic Bottle", for a man in your condition.

Japanese: （し‥‥しまった！）
English: ("The Magic Bottle"...? I hope it doesn't have a child-proof cap on it...)

Japanese: エリス先生は、リッパな 絵本作家‥‥それだけよ！
English: She was a wonderful author and illustrator... That's all! I swear!

Japanese: ‥‥これは、エリス先生が 身につけていたものです。
English: Ms. Deauxnim had this charm with her at all times.

Japanese: げええええええええええええッ！ あなた、それは‥‥！
English: Kaaargh! Where did you...!?

Japanese: ‥‥ただの《お守り》には 見えません。
English: This isn't some cheap good luck charm, either.

Japanese: じゃ‥‥‥じゃあ なんだって言うんだいッ！
English: Th-Then... What do you think it is, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: そ、そ、その護符（ごふ）に、 いったい、どんなイミが！
English: Wh-What could a talisman like that possibly mean?

Japanese: “ごふ”って いうんですか、これ。
English: It's a "talisman", is it? Interesting...

Japanese: ‥‥うううう‥‥
English: Oops!

Japanese: コイツが、物語っているんです。 彼女が、何者か‥‥？
English: This thing speaks volumes about who Ms. Deauxnim really was.

Japanese: この護符と、ある証拠品によって、 エリス先生の正体がハッキリする！
English: This talisman and one other item unequivocally prove Ms. Deauxnim's true identity!

Japanese: いかがですか！　ビキニさん！
English: Well!?

Japanese: ‥‥アンタ、葉桜院の 《お守り》を買ったほうがいいね。
English: You really ought to think about buying one of the charms we sell here, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: さもないと‥‥
English: Otherwise...

Japanese: 暗い夜道で、小さな オバサンに襲われるよッ！
English: You just might find yourself jumped on by a little old lady on a dark mountain path!

Japanese: （何をやっているんだ！ カンタンな話じゃないか！）
English: (What am I doing!? The piece of evidence I need to show Bikini is obvious!)

Japanese: そ、そんなの‥‥ただの お守りだよ！　返しなさい！
English: That's... That's just a plain old good luck charm. So please, just give it back!

Japanese: ‥‥そうは行かないんです！
English: Oh, no! I don't think so!

Japanese: ‥‥今はカレーで見えませんが‥‥
English: The painting is obscured by this gravy stain at the moment...

Japanese: この掛け軸には、倉院流霊媒道の 家元の姿が描かれているそうです。
English: But this scroll shows the Master of the Kurain Channeling Technique.

Japanese: それを証明してるのは、 上に描かれている《紋》です。
English: The crest at the top is a sign of that honorable title.

Japanese: 被害者の、この護符にも‥‥ 同じものが刻みこまれていますね。
English: The very same crest that adorns the talisman the victim carried with her!

Japanese: ああああッ！
English: Aaaah!

Japanese: この《紋》こそが‥‥家元を あらわす“アカシ”なのです。
English: This crest is reserved for the Master of Kurain.

Japanese: だ‥‥ダレがそんなコトを！ お、オバサンは、し、知らないよ！
English: Who on Earth told you that nonsense!? I-I've got no idea what you're talking about!

Japanese: 新しい倉院流の家元‥‥ 綾里　真宵が、そう証言しました！
English: The new Master, Maya Fey, told me!
Wh...

Japanese: なんだって！
English: WHAT!?

Japanese: あの‥‥あの、修験者さまが‥‥ 舞子さまの‥‥？
English: Y-You mean that acolyte... is Misty Fey's...

Japanese: むすめさんです。
English: Daughter? Yes.

Japanese: ホント‥‥なのかい？
English: Are you sure?

Japanese: ええ。‥‥今となっては、 ただひとりの後継者です。
English: Yes. In fact, since her first daughter passed away, Maya is Misty Fey's sole successor.

Japanese: まいこさまああああああああッ！
English: Mystic Mistyyyyyyy!

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 葉桜院　境内
English: February 9 Hazakura Temple Courtyard

Japanese: （‥‥ビキニさんは、 ここで事件を目撃した）
English: (This is where Sister Bikini witnessed the incident.)

Japanese: （ウソをついているとは 思えないけど‥‥）
English: (It's hard to imagine she was lying on the stand, so...)

Japanese: （まだ、他に手がかりが 眠っているんだろうか‥‥？）
English: (...maybe there are some clues that have yet to be found.)

Japanese: 綾里　供子像が持っている、 あの七支刀‥‥
English: The Shichishito. It's there in Mystic Ami's right hand.

Japanese: 今日の法廷で、あれが凶器では ないと立証されたらしい。
English: But it was proven in court today that it wasn't the murder weapon.

Japanese: とりあえず、罪作りなカタナで あることは、まちがいないな。
English: Given the design, it must've been used for some pretty gruesome things in the past...

Japanese: 境内には、いくつか 灯ろうが並んでいる。
English: There are a few of these stone lanterns scattered around in this courtyard.

Japanese: 街灯がない山の中では、小さい 灯りでも、とても明るく感じる。
English: Without the city glare to compete with, I imagine they would seem a lot brighter.

Japanese: あやめさんがいなくなって‥‥ ビキニさんが灯を入れるのかな。
English: I guess Sister Bikini must light them now, since Iris can't be here.

Japanese: ぼくたちがお世話になっている 葉桜院の本堂だ。
English: That building up there is the Main Hall. It was where we were staying.

Japanese: 急な斜面に建てられているため、 こんなことになってるけど‥‥
English: It sits kind of funny because it was built on a very steep slope.

Japanese: 目の上に見ている部屋は、 山門側から地つづきの、１階だ。
English: If you enter from the Main Gate side, these rooms above me are on the ground floor.

Japanese: カドの部屋は、亡くなった エリス先生が泊まっていたらしい。
English: Apparently, Ms. Deauxnim was staying in the corner room.

Japanese: あそこから、突き落とされた ‥‥のだろうか？
English: I guess that's where she was pushed from that night...

Japanese: 吾童山中は、かなり 雪がつもるから、
English: They get quite a bit of snow up here on Eagle Mountain.

Japanese: スキーは遊び道具ではなく、 移動手段なのかもしれない。
English: Around here, skis aren't just for fun. They're tools for getting around.

Japanese: ‥‥ソリは、ビキニさんが 遊ぶのだろう。
English: But the sled... Now that's gotta be for Bikini's own personal entertainment!

Japanese: 石垣の上は、山門に通じている。
English: The Main Gate's on the other side of that stone wall.

Japanese: 段差は３メートル近くある。 山の斜面が、それだけ急なのだ。
English: The difference in elevation is almost 10 feet. That's how steep this hillside is!

Japanese: エリス先生の身体は、３メートル ぐらいの高さから落ちたらしい。
English: And that's also the height the body fell from, according to the autopsy.

Japanese: もしかしたら、あそこかも しれない‥‥かな？
English: Is it possible that Ms. Deauxnim was pushed from the top of the wall...?

Japanese: エリス先生が持っていた、ツエ。
English: Ms. Deauxnim's staff...

Japanese: たしかに、アタマのところに 水晶がついていた。
English: I definitely remember there being a crystal sphere attached to the staff.

Japanese: その水晶は、矢張が橋の近くで 拾ったらしいけど‥‥
English: I guess Larry eventually found the sphere near the foot of Dusky Bridge, but...

Japanese: どうして、そんなところに 落ちていたんだろう‥‥？
English: ...what was it doing all the way out there...?

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 山小屋　極楽庵
English: February 9 Heavenly Hall

Japanese: （だれもいない‥‥）
English: (Looks like no one's here...)

Japanese: 矢張ぃっ！
English: Larryyy!

Japanese: マシスぅっ！
English: Lauriiice!

Japanese: （行き場がなくなって、ここに ひそんでいると思ったのに‥‥）
English: (I was sure he'd be hiding here...)

Japanese: （また、あとで来てみるか）
English: (Guess I'll try again later.)

Japanese: ワラぶきの屋根に、 雪がふんわりとつもっている。
English: The snow is really piled up on the straw roof.

Japanese: おとぎ話の世界みたいだ。 ‥‥屋根だけ見れば。
English: It's otherworldly... Like something out of a fairy tale. ...Well, the roof part anyway.

Japanese: 《極楽庵》と書かれた 一枚板が、かかげられている。
English: The sign says, "Heavenly Hall".

Japanese: こんなサムいところで 一晩すごしたら‥‥
English: I guess it's pretty fitting. If you spent a night in this little freezing shack...

Japanese: たしかに、極楽あたりに 行けそうな気がする。
English: ...you'd probably find yourself at the pearly gates by morning.

Japanese: ‥‥せっかくのいいフンイキが 台なしになっている。
English: That stuff is really ruining the beautiful atmosphere around here.

Japanese: この、なんとも イタイタしい感じは‥‥
English: It's so inappropriate.

Japanese: あえてたとえるならば、 ビキニをつけたビキニさん、かな。
English: Like Sister Bikini in a bikini! *shudder* ...I've gotta think of the kittens...

Japanese: これをのぼっていくと、 おぼろ橋のたもとにつく。
English: These steps lead up to the foot of Dusky Bridge.

Japanese: おりて来るのはラクでいいけど‥‥ 帰るのが、めんどくさいな。
English: Coming down is a cinch, but going back up doesn't look like a walk in the park.

Japanese: ‥‥川がなぜ下流に向かうのか、 水のキモチがわかる気がする。
English: I'm starting to understand why rivers always want to flow downstream, not up.

Japanese: あれは‥‥おぼろ橋だな。 遠いけど、ハッキリ見える。
English: That's Dusky Bridge up there. It's amazing how clearly you can see it from so far away.

Japanese: 矢張のヤツ、小屋の窓から あの事件を目撃したのだろう。
English: Larry supposedly saw everything through the shack's window.

Japanese: このへんは明かりがないから、 夜はまっ暗だろうけど‥‥
English: Not much light gets down here, so it must be pitch black at night...

Japanese: あの晩にかぎっては、 橋がバリバリ燃えてたからなあ。
English: But this area was probably illuminated that night because of the burning bridge above.

Japanese: これが、ウワサの吾童川か。 事件当夜、ぼくも流されてみた。
English: This is the famous Eagle River. I got to try out its rough rapids the other night.

Japanese: 《かなりの急流だ》とは 聞いていたけど‥‥
English: They say it has a really strong current.

Japanese: たしかに、そうとうなパワー、 そしてスピードだったな、アレは。
English: And they weren't kidding. It's both powerful and fast-flowing.

Japanese: よく助かったな、ぼく。
English: I was dead lucky... I mean lucky not to be dead!

Japanese: 小屋のドテっぱらに、 好き勝手にラクガキしてある。
English: Look at this graffiti, scrawled right on the front of the shack.

Japanese: ‥‥でも、アイツの似顔絵、 意外と似ているんだよな。
English: At least he managed to get a pretty good likeness.

Japanese: 考えてみれば、あいつには ゲージュツが合うのかもしれない。
English: When I really think about it, I guess art kind of suits him.

Japanese: 存在自体がアートだからな。 ‥‥あるイミ。
English: I mean, his very existence is an art... of sorts.

Japanese: （今はまだ、証拠が 足りないのかもしれない）
English: (I don't think I have enough evidence yet...)

Japanese: （もう少し調査をして、 材料を集めてみるか‥‥）
English: (I should investigate and gather some more clues before I try again...)

Japanese: ‥なるほどくん‥
English: ...Mr. Nick...

Japanese: ‥これ以上つづけると、 ココロがこわれてしまいます‥
English: ...If you push yourself any more, your soul will shatter...

Japanese: ‥もう一度、冷静になって 考えなおしましょう‥
English: ...Please calm down, collect your thoughts, and try again...

Japanese: （‥‥しまった！ シッパイしすぎたか‥‥）
English: (Nnrgh! I've made too many mistakes!)
